sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Lecce have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins and 3 losses, struggling to score consistently with only 3 goals in those games. Como, on the other hand, have been more impressive with 2 wins, including a dominant 5-1 victory over Torino, though they’ve also suffered heavy defeats like the 4-0 loss to Inter. Como’s attacking output of 7 goals in their last 5 games gives them an edge in form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Como have dominated Lecce, winning both matches with clean sheets (2-0 and 3-0). These results highlight Como’s ability to exploit Lecce’s defensive vulnerabilities, especially in the final third, where Lecce have struggled to create big chances.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Lecce, sitting 15th with 16 points, have struggled offensively, scoring just 11 goals (19th in the league) and creating the fewest big chances (20th). Como, positioned 7th with 24 points, have a stronger attack (19 goals) and boast key players like Nico Paz (5 goals, 5 assists). Defensively, Como are also more solid, conceding fewer goals (12 vs Lecce’s 19).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Lecce will miss key midfielders like Medon Berisha and Lameck Banda, weakening their creativity. Como’s Nico Paz has been in excellent form, ranking high in goals and assists, though they’ll miss forward Álvaro Morata. Como’s depth and individual quality give them an advantage.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Lecce’s high corner count (5.5 per game, 3rd in the league) could be a weapon, but their low conversion rate of big chances (30%, 16th) limits their threat. Como excel in final third entries and late-game scoring (25% of goals after the 76th minute), which could test Lecce’s tiring defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Como’s superior form, head-to-head dominance, and attacking firepower, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 45% away win probability. Lecce’s home advantage at Via del Mare might not be enough to overcome their offensive struggles and Como’s confidence. A low-scoring game is likely due to historical trends of under 2.5 goals in head-to-heads.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Lecce and Como at the Via del Mare saw Como take a deserved lead with a goal in the 20th minute by Nico Paz. Como dominated possession and created more threatening opportunities, translating their control into a tangible advantage. Lecce, on the other hand, struggled to impose themselves, managing only minimal attempts on goal and failing to create significant chances. The halftime scoreline of 0-1 reflects Como's superior play, though Lecce showed some resilience in defense despite being under pressure for much of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Como showcased a strong performance in the first half, controlling the ball for the majority of the time and frequently entering the final third. Their ability to penetrate Lecce’s penalty area and win possessions in dangerous zones highlights their attacking intent. They also converted one of their chances into a goal, with Nico Paz proving clinical. Lecce, however, were on the back foot, with limited entries into Como’s penalty area and a lack of creativity in the final third. Despite winning a higher percentage of duels, they couldn’t translate this into meaningful attacking output, leaving them chasing the game at halftime.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and moments
halftimeSections.text: Nico Paz was the standout performer for Como, scoring the decisive goal with a well-taken left-footed strike and earning the highest rating among players in the first half. Mergim Vojvoda also played a crucial role with an assist, demonstrating Como’s ability to combine effectively in attack. For Lecce, players like Ylber Ramadani and Antonino Gallo showed defensive grit with interceptions and tackles, but their contributions were largely limited to containing Como’s pressure. Mohamed Kaba offered a rare spark in attack for Lecce, though it wasn’t enough to trouble Como’s defense significantly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung heavily in favor of Como early on, as they mounted consistent pressure and maintained attacking spells for extended periods. Lecce had brief moments of promise at the start and towards the end of the half, but these were quickly overshadowed by Como’s relentless forward push. The away side showed greater intent in building attacks, particularly around the time of their goal, while Lecce struggled to sustain any meaningful rhythm in their offensive play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Lecce will need to adopt a more aggressive approach to overturn the deficit, likely pushing forward with greater numbers and taking risks. This could open up spaces for Como to exploit on the counter, especially given their attacking cohesion shown in the first half. Como, meanwhile, may look to manage the game by maintaining possession and striking on the break, capitalizing on any gaps left by Lecce. The game could see more goals if Lecce’s urgency leads to defensive lapses, but Como appear poised to hold their lead unless Lecce can find a creative spark.
